text: The surgeon inserts an optical trocar using the modified Scandinavian technique at the Inter-Anglican crease, followed by placing a 5mm working port in the suprapubic region.
The surgeon uses a gut-friendly grasp technique to manipulate the intestines and locate the appendix.
The surgeon visually follows the tinea coli along the colon to locate the base of the appendix, regardless of its position, whether retrocecal or subserosal.
The appendix is observed extending retrocecally, positioned behind the cecum.
The surgeon identifies the white ureter running along the pelvic side wall, with the ovarian vessels visible above it.
The surgeon visually identifies the acutely inflamed appendix, characterized by redness and swelling, within the surgical field.
The surgeon inserts a 5mm working port in the left mid-clavicular line, positioned midway between the optical port and the suprapubic port.
The surgeon holds the ligature Maryland instrument in the left hand and the grasping instrument in the right hand, preparing to manipulate the appendix.

The surgeon uses an energy device, such as a harmonic or ligature, held in the left hand through the suprapubic port to dissect the meso-appendix, aligning with the long axis of the retrocecal appendix for easier dissection.
The surgeon dissects the mesoappendix, staying close to the long axis of the acutely inflamed appendix, using instruments inserted through the suprapubic port.
The surgeon ensures that no fatty tissue or remnants of the mesoappendix are left attached to the acutely inflamed appendix during the dissection.
It hinders with the removal of the appendix through the working port, especially if the appendix is slim and can be removed with a five mm port.
The surgeon uses a LigaClip to ligate the base of the appendix, securing it before removal.
The surgeon replaces the suprapubic working port with a Canon instrument, preparing for further steps in the appendectomy procedure.
There are many ways to ligate the base of the appendix. There are my videos explaining different ways.
And here we are done with this procedure.
